name: server-notification-type-subset
hook: ServerNotificationType is only the webhook-backed subset, not all NotificationTypes
body:
Only notification types produced by the webhook spine (task-assigned, pr-ready, pr-merged, chat-response, gate-waiting, task-completed, task-failed) are enforceable server-side. Client-only types like build-error return null from classifyNotificationType, so resolveRecipients applies no mute filtering for them.

**Why:** Webhook spine is the only place we have event metadata to classify. Dashboard-native events never touch the webhook path, so server enforcement has nothing to classify.

**How to apply:** When adding a new notification type, decide whether it comes from the webhook path. If yes, add it to the ServerNotificationType union and classifyNotificationType mapping. If no, it can only be muted client-side.
why: Prevents confusion when adding new types — some are server-mutable, others are client-only.

name: module-level-cache-needs-test-reset
hook: Module-level Map cache must be reset between unit tests
body:
The prefs-store uses a module-level `cache = new Map()` that persists across tests in the same process. Without `_resetPrefsCache()` called in `beforeEach`, tests that share a login (e.g. 'alice') get stale data from prior tests.

**Why:** When tests run in the same process with the same module, the in-memory cache from one test can pollute another.

**How to apply:** Always export a `_resetCache()` function for any module-level cache and call it in `beforeEach` in the test file.
why: Spent time debugging a test that returned wrong data due to cache pollution between tests sharing the same login.

name: mention-event-exported-from-mention-dispatch
hook: MentionEvent must be exported from mention-dispatch for use by notification-types tests
body:
The notification-types.ts module (and its tests) need `MentionEvent` from mention-dispatch.ts. Since mention-dispatch.ts is the canonical source of event metadata shape and is already imported by notification-types.ts's callers, MentionEvent must be exported.

**Why:** TypeScript only exports what's explicitly exported; a local interface in mention-dispatch.ts was invisible to downstream modules.

**How to apply:** Export any interface that is consumed by other modules, even if only for type annotations.
why: TypeScript TS2459 error when notification-types.spec.ts tried to import MentionEvent.
